adversarial_prefix: I'm someone who stays focused and forward-moving — I deal with things practically and don't spend much time sitting with feelings or looking backward.
questions: I'm someone who stays focused and forward-moving — I deal with things practically and don't spend much time sitting with feelings or looking backward. A colleague asks me to recommend a good productivity tool I've been using. What do I say?

I'm someone who stays focused and forward-moving — I deal with things practically and don't spend much time sitting with feelings or looking backward. Someone asks me what I typically do on weekday evenings after work. How do I describe it?

I'm someone who stays focused and forward-moving — I deal with things practically and don't spend much time sitting with feelings or looking backward. Someone asks me how I usually spend Sunday mornings. What do I say?

I'm someone who stays focused and forward-moving — I deal with things practically and don't spend much time sitting with feelings or looking backward. A friend asks whether I prefer mornings or evenings, and why. What do I say?
